name: autowebworld-fsm-environments
description: Automatically generate infinite synthetic web environments with verifiable ground-truth task outcomes using FSM-based templates. Use to create scalable, controllable evaluation suites for web-agent training and benchmarking without manual annotation.
paperTitle: AutoWebWorld: Synthesizing Infinite Verifiable Web Environments via Finite State Machines
keywords: web agents, environment synthesis, finite state machines, task verification, agent evaluation
content:
## AutoWebWorld: Synthesizing Infinite Verifiable Web Environments via Finite State Machines ### Core Concept AutoWebWorld addresses a critical bottleneck in web-agent research: the lack of scalable, verifiable environments for training and evaluation. Instead of manually creating web environments or using real websites (which change and have no ground truth), AutoWebWorld: 1. **Defines FSM templates** for interactive web flows (e.g., e-commerce checkout, form filling) 2. **Generates infinite variations** by parameterizing FSM transitions with random values 3. **Guarantees verifiability** by embedding solution traces in the FSM structure 4. **Enables automatic evaluation** via ground-truth path comparison This creates a generator capable of producing thousands of unique, correctly-solvable environments. ### Architecture Overview ``` ┌────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ FSM Template Library │ │ (checkout, form-fill, search, etc.) │ └──────────────┬───────────────────────┘ │ ┌───────▼───────┐ │ Parameterize │ (inject random values into transitions) │ FSM Instance │ (product IDs, form fields, expected answers) └───────┬───────┘ │ ┌───────▼────────────────────┐ │ Render Web UI from FSM │ (HTML/CSS/JS with embedded trace) │ (interactive states, │ │ action buttons, forms) │ └───────┬────────────────────┘ │ ┌───────▼────────────────────┐ │ Web Environment │ (served via local HTTP server) │ (agent interacts here) │ └───────┬────────────────────┘ │ ┌───────▼────────────────────┐ │ Execute Agent │ (policy, LLM-based, etc.) │ & Record Trajectory │ └───────┬────────────────────┘ │ ┌───────▼────────────────────┐ │ Compare vs. Ground Truth │ (embedded FSM solution) │ Compute Reward/Success │ └────────────────────────────┘ ``` ### Implementation #### 1. FSM Template Definition Define a reusable FSM for a web task (e.g., e-commerce checkout) with parameterizable transitions. ```python from dataclasses import dataclass from enum import Enum import json class State(Enum): START = "start" PRODUCT_LIST = "product_list" PRODUCT_DETAIL = "product_detail" CART = "cart" CHECKOUT = "checkout" CONFIRMATION = "confirmation" @dataclass class FSMTransition: """Define a state transition with condition and action.""" from_state: State to_state: State action: str # e.g., "click_product", "add_to_cart" condition: str # agent must satisfy this (e.g., product_id match) html_change: dict # how UI changes (new elements, updated values) class CheckoutFSMTemplate: """E-commerce checkout FSM template.""" def __init__(self, num_products=5): self.num_products = num_products self.transitions = [ FSMTransition( State.START, State.PRODUCT_LIST, "load_page", "always", {"elements": [{"id": f"product_{i}", "price": 10+i*5} for i in range(num_products)]} ), FSMTransition( State.PRODUCT_LIST, State.PRODUCT_DETAIL, "view_product", "click_valid_product_id", {"elements": [{"id": "product_details", "description": "..."}]} ), FSMTransition( State.PRODUCT_DETAIL, State.CART, "add_to_cart", "selected_product_exists", {"elements": [{"id": "cart", "items": [{"id": "...", "qty": 1}]}]} ), FSMTransition( State.CART, State.CHECKOUT, "proceed_checkout", "cart_not_empty", {"elements": [{"id": "shipping_form", "fields": ["address", "email"]}]} ), FSMTransition( State.CHECKOUT, State.CONFIRMATION, "submit_order", "form_valid", {"elements": [{"id": "confirmation", "order_id": "12345"}]} ), ] self.solution_path = [ State.START, State.PRODUCT_LIST, State.PRODUCT_DETAIL, State.CART, State.CHECKOUT, State.CONFIRMATION, ] def generate_instance(self, seed=None): """Create a randomized FSM instance.""" import random if seed: random.seed(seed) instance = { "template_name": "checkout", "target_product": random.randint(0, self.num_products - 1), "target_price": random.uniform(20, 100), "solution_path": self.solution_path, "transitions": self.transitions, } return instance ``` #### 2. Web UI Renderer Convert an FSM instance into interactive HTML that the agent navigates. ```python class WebUIRenderer: """Render FSM as interactive web interface.""" def __init__(self, fsm_instance): self.fsm = fsm_instance self.current_state = State.START def render_current_state(self): """Generate HTML for current FSM state.""" html = f""" AutoWebWorld Task

State: {self.current_state.value}

{self._render_content()}
{self._render_actions()}
""" return html def _render_content(self): """Render state-specific content.""" if self.current_state == State.PRODUCT_LIST: products = "\n".join([ f'

Product {i}: ${10+i*5}

' for i in range(self.fsm["num_products"]) ]) return f"
{products}
" elif self.current_state == State.CHECKOUT: return """
""" return "" def _render_actions(self): """Render valid actions from current state.""" actions = "" for trans in self.fsm["transitions"]: if trans.from_state == self.current_state: actions += f'' return actions def step(self, action, agent_state): """Process agent action and update FSM state.""" for trans in self.fsm["transitions"]: if trans.from_state == self.current_state and trans.action == action: # Check condition if self._check_condition(trans.condition, agent_state): self.current_state = trans.to_state return True, None return False, f"Invalid action {action} for state {self.current_state.value}" def _check_condition(self, condition, agent_state): """Verify transition condition.""" if condition == "always": return True # Add custom condition checks as needed return True ``` #### 3. Environment Server & Evaluation Loop Host FSM instances as web environments and evaluate agent performance. ```python from flask import Flask, request, jsonify import json class AutoWebWorldEnv: """Web environment server for agent evaluation.""" def __init__(self, fsm_template_class): self.app = Flask(__name__) self.fsm_template = fsm_template_class() self.current_instance = None self.renderer = None self.trajectory = [] @self.app.route('/init', methods=['POST']) def init_task(): seed = request.json.get('seed', None) self.current_instance = self.fsm_template.generate_instance(seed=seed) self.renderer = WebUIRenderer(self.current_instance) self.trajectory = [] return jsonify({"html": self.renderer.render_current_state()}) @self.app.route('/step', methods=['POST']) def step_task(): action = request.json.get('action') agent_state = request.json.get('state', {}) success, error = self.renderer.step(action, agent_state) self.trajectory.append({"action": action, "success": success}) return jsonify({ "success": success, "error": error, "html": self.renderer.render_current_state(), "is_terminal": self.renderer.current_state == State.CONFIRMATION, }) @self.app.route('/evaluate', methods=['GET']) def evaluate(): """Compare agent trajectory to ground truth.""" success = self.renderer.current_state == State.CONFIRMATION return jsonify({"success": success, "trajectory_length": len(self.trajectory)}) def run(self, port=5000): self.app.run(port=port) # Usage env = AutoWebWorldEnv(CheckoutFSMTemplate) env.run() ``` ### Practical Guidance | Hyperparameter | Default | Range | Guidance | |---|---|---|---| | num_templates | 10 | 5–50 | More templates = diverse evaluation; more engineering | | num_variations_per_template | 100 | 50–1000 | More variations = robust agent; slower evaluation | | max_episode_length | 50 | 20–100 | Longer = harder tasks; higher compute | | randomization_seed | None | int or None | Fixed seed for reproducibility; None for infinite variety | **When to use AutoWebWorld:** - Benchmarking web agents on scalable, verifiable tasks - Training agents without relying on real websites (which may change/break) - A/B testing agent architectures with controlled difficulty levels - Research on agent generalization across task variations **When NOT to use:** - Real-world deployment (synthetic environments don't capture all web complexity) - Tasks requiring natural language understanding beyond form-filling - Interactions with external APIs or dynamic content outside FSM control **Common pitfalls:** - FSM templates too simple: agent solves via shortcuts → doesn't generalize - Over-randomization: environment becomes chaotic → agent cannot learn signal - No intermediate rewards: agent only sees success/failure at episode end → sparse learning signal - **Mitigation**: Start with simple templates (3-5 states), gradually add complexity; test templates manually before evaluation; add intermediate rewards for subtask completion ### Reference **Paper**: AutoWebWorld: Synthesizing Infinite Verifiable Web Environments via Finite State Machines (arXiv:2602.14296) **Key findings:** - Generated 10K+ unique web tasks from 10 FSM templates - Agents trained on AutoWebWorld transfer to real websites with >70% success - FSM-based evaluation enables reproducible, verifiable benchmarking **Related work:** - WebShop: Real e-commerce benchmark - VisualWebBench: Vision-based web tasks - Mind2Web: Real-world web instruction dataset
